rationale: 14 blocking findings across security, API contract deviations, and error handling gaps. Diff is oversized (1571 lines across 21 files) limiting single-pass review coverage. Spec deviations: missing /status polling endpoint, initiate response shape mismatch, disconnect revokes wrong token, no rate limiting, missing has_authorization method. Security: silent exception swallowing masks auth failures, Redis connection errors unguarded, empty config defaults allow misconfigured deployments.

business_review.notes: Returns authorization_url but state not returned; discarded as _state
Implemented as GET /status with no state param; returns DB check not Redis polling
Implemented as DELETE; revokes access_token instead of refresh_token

business_review.item: Tokens encrypted at rest using EncryptionFactory
One token set per user (PK on user_id)
business_review.notes: GoogleOAuthToken uses user_id as PK with upsert

business_review.item: GoogleDocDatasourceProcessor fetches token and raises on auth errors
business_review.notes: Fetches token but catches all exceptions with fallback to default auth instead of raising

business_review.item: Datasource creation checks authorization (401 if missing)
business_review.notes: index_knowledge_base_google_doc has no authorization check; has_authorization method missing

business_review.item: Rate limiting on /initiate (10 req/min per user)
Callback fetches user email from Google
business_review.notes: handle_callback passes email='' literal; no userinfo call
Method does not exist in GoogleOAuthTokenService

standards_review.notes: All commits follow EPMCDME-13222 prefix pattern
Ruff checks passed across all modified files
Silent exception swallowing masks authorization failures; Redis connection errors propagate as 500 with stack traces; empty string config defaults allow misconfigured deployments

findings.title: Silent auth failure fallback masks user token expiry
findings.problem: Bare except Exception in _init_loader and check_google_doc catches GoogleOAuthRefreshError (stale credentials) and silently falls back to default service-account auth instead of surfacing the authorization error to the user
findings.impact: User whose OAuth token expired will unknowingly access documents via shared service account rather than being told their credentials need re-authorization; bypasses per-user access control
findings.recommendation: Catch GoogleOAuthTokenError for expected no-token case with fallback; re-raise GoogleOAuthRefreshError so caller knows credentials are stale

findings.title: Redis connection error unguarded in initiate_oauth
findings.problem: create_redis_client() and setex() called without try/except; ConnectionError propagates as unhandled 500 with internal stack trace exposed
findings.impact: When Redis unavailable, /initiate returns 500 with full traceback including file paths and library versions; information disclosure
findings.recommendation: Wrap Redis operations in try/except, raise HTTPException(503, 'Service temporarily unavailable') on ConnectionError

findings.title: Empty string OAuth config defaults silently degrade
findings.problem: GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET default to empty string, not None; code checking truthiness treats misconfigured deployment as 'OAuth not configured' rather than failing fast
findings.impact: Production deployment with missing env vars will silently degrade OAuth flow instead of raising startup error; hard to diagnose
findings.recommendation: Add @model_validator to check both are non-empty or log warning/raise at startup

findings.title: Missing /status polling endpoint per spec
findings.problem: Spec requires GET /v1/google-docs/oauth/status/{state} that reads Redis result and validates state ownership; implemented endpoint has no state param and checks DB only
findings.impact: Frontend cannot poll OAuth completion; spec's pending/success/error flow and 403 state-ownership guard entirely absent
findings.recommendation: Implement GET /v1/google-docs/oauth/status/{state} per spec: read codemie:google_oauth:result:{state} from Redis, validate user owns state, return 202/200/400

findings.title: Callback never writes result to Redis for polling
findings.problem: handle_callback does not write codemie:google_oauth:result:{state} (TTL 300s) after token storage; polling endpoint has no data source
findings.impact: Frontend polling /status will never find completion result; OAuth flow appears stuck
findings.recommendation: After store_token succeeds, write result to Redis: self._redis.setex(f'oauth_result:{state}', 300, json.dumps({'email': email}))

findings.title: Initiate response discards state token
findings.problem: State token generated and stored in Redis but not returned to caller (_state variable discarded); spec requires state in response for frontend to poll with
findings.impact: Frontend cannot correlate /initiate call with /status polling; cannot implement polling flow
findings.recommendation: Return InitiateResponse(authorization_url=auth_url, state=state) with state field added to model

findings.title: Disconnect revokes access_token instead of refresh_token
findings.problem: Spec Step 1 says revoke refresh_token; implementation fetches and revokes access_token; also uses DELETE not POST as spec requires
findings.impact: Access token revoked but refresh token remains valid; user could still refresh and regain access after disconnect

findings.title: Missing has_authorization method for upfront checks
findings.problem: Spec defines has_authorization(user_id) for datasource creation endpoint to return 401 before allowing datasource creation; method does not exist
findings.impact: No way to check authorization upfront; users can create datasources then get cryptic errors at index time
findings.recommendation: Add has_authorization(user_id) -> bool: return self.repository.get_by_user_id(user_id) is not None

findings.title: Missing guard for None refresh_token after code exchange
findings.problem: credentials.refresh_token is None if Google omits it (prompt!=consent first-time flow); stored encrypted as None; next refresh call fails with decrypt error
findings.impact: OAuth flow succeeds but tokens cannot refresh; appears broken after 1 hour when access token expires
findings.recommendation: After fetch_token: if not credentials.refresh_token: raise GoogleOAuthTokenError('No refresh token; ensure prompt=consent')

findings.title: Empty string access_token builds invalid OAuth credentials
findings.problem: access_token checked with 'is not None'; empty string passes check, creates Credentials(token=''), API call fails with 401
findings.impact: Token fetch that returns empty string (decrypt error?) silently fails at Google API call instead of being caught early
findings.recommendation: Change to 'if access_token:' (truthiness check) to guard empty string

findings.title: Encrypted token may exceed DB column length
findings.problem: access_token max_length=2048; encrypted token with AES-GCM/Fernet overhead may exceed limit on long Google tokens
findings.impact: DB insert raises DataError truncating ciphertext; token storage silently fails; OAuth flow broken
findings.recommendation: Increase Field(max_length=4096) or use Text column for access_token and refresh_token

findings.title: GoogleOAuthRefreshError uses HTTP 401 instead of 502
findings.problem: Spec requires 502 for transient token refresh failures (network errors) vs 401 for expired authorization; both collapsed into single 401 exception
findings.impact: Frontend cannot distinguish 'retry later' (502) from 're-authorize' (401); poor UX on transient failures
findings.recommendation: Use code=502 for GoogleOAuthRefreshError; or split into AuthorizationExpired (401) vs TokenRefreshFailed (502)

findings.title: Datasource creation has no authorization check
findings.problem: index_knowledge_base_google_doc endpoint not modified; spec requires upfront authorization check with 401 if user not authorized
findings.impact: Users can create Google Docs datasources before connecting OAuth; datasource fails at index time with unclear error
findings.recommendation: Add check before datasource creation: if not token_service.has_authorization(user.id): raise ExtendedHTTPException(401, 'Google Docs authorization required')

findings.title: No rate limiting on /initiate endpoint
findings.problem: Spec requires 10 requests/minute per user; no rate limiting decorator or middleware applied
findings.impact: User can spam /initiate generating unlimited Redis state keys and authorization URLs; abuse vector
findings.recommendation: Add rate limiting: @limiter.limit('10/minute') decorator on initiate_oauth
